title: Past-only latent degradation slope and cliff features
summary: Recent F1 tire-degradation work models pace as a latent state with stint resets and time-varying degradation; the pit-stop DL paper also reports gains from sequence-aware models.
rationale: Recent F1 tire-degradation work models pace as a latent state with stint resets and time-varying degradation; the pit-stop DL paper also reports gains from sequence-aware models. Your best features are static window heuristics, so a simpler tabular version is to estimate online degradation state, slope, and acceleration within each driver-race-stint using only prior laps.
implementation_hint: For each (Year,Race,Driver,Stint), build strictly lagged features: 1-3 lap rolling means of LapTime_Delta and Cumulative_Degradation, exponential-weighted slope, second-difference 'cliff' indicators, residual-to-stint-trend, and time-since-last-local-minimum pace. Reset at PitStop/Stint boundaries. Feed these into the existing booster rather than switching fully to an RNN.
expected_effect: Should improve recall near true stop windows by detecting accelerating tire drop-off that static tyre-age ratios miss.
risk: If row order is not perfectly chronological inside each driver-race sequence, leakage or noisy lags will hurt CV; needs strict sorting and fold-safe computation.
